17.reason: // Missing else clause to handle case when there are digits after the first non-zero digit in fractional part (e.g., 0.0102). Also, exponent calculation is incorrect for numbers like 0.001.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  // Missing else clause to handle numbers like 0.00123 where more than one digit follows the first non-zero digit after decimal
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  // Missing else clause to handle case when there are digits after the first non-zero digit in fractional part (e.g., 0.0102). Also, exponent calculation is incorrect for numbers like 0.001.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  // Missing else clause to handle case when there are digits after the first non-zero digit in fractional part (e.g., 0.0123). Also, exponent calculation is incorrect: should be -(i - k) or similar, not 1 - i.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  // Missing else clause to handle case when there are more digits after the first non-zero digit in fractional part (e.g., 0.0123). Also, exponent calculation is incorrect: should be -(i - k) or similar, not 1 - i.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  // Missing else clause to handle case when there are digits after the first non-zero digit in fractional part (e.g., 0.0123)

20.reason: // Incorrectly prints integer part as digit even when leading zero exists (violates problem constraint that if more than one digit before decimal, first cannot be zero). Also, if input has only one digit before '.', this branch shouldn't run unless k != 1.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  // Should print character s[0], not convert to int and print as %d (e.g., '5' becomes 53)
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  // Incorrectly prints integer part as digit even when leading zero exists (violates problem constraint that if more than one digit before decimal, first cannot be zero). Also, if input has only one digit before '.', this branch shouldn't run unless k != 1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  // Incorrectly prints integer part as digit even when leading digit is '0' (violates problem constraint that if more than one digit before decimal, first can't be zero, but also mishandles base format)
